authoritative_sources.title: Code of Conduct
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 4A's is the U.S. advertising agency trade association; its public code defines agency ethics, confidentiality, proprietary-information handling, and truthful conduct constraints that limit production coordination disclosure and approval behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Confidentiality, agency conduct, truthful handling, and client-work-product protection
used_for: confidentiality hold and disclosure refusal triggers
agency work product and client file handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires production handoff outputs to protect client files, pitch work, production assets, vendor bids, and unreleased creative while avoiding unsupported approval language.

authoritative_sources.title: ANA/4A's Guidelines for Agency Search
authoritative_sources.publisher: Association of National Advertisers / American Association of Advertising Agencies
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ANA and 4A's jointly represent advertiser and agency operating perspectives; their guidance defines agency-client scope, role clarity, decision makers, RFI/RFP and pitch workflow, confidentiality agreements, deliverables, bottleneck handling, and relationship-management checkpoints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Agency engagement, role clarity, client decision makers, production-adjacent handoffs, bottlenecks, and relationship checkpoints
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to require clear scope, role, owner, decision maker, deliverable, confidentiality, and checkpoint evidence before routing production work across agency teams or vendors.

authoritative_sources.title: Best Practice Guidance: Agency Search Agreements (AKA New Business NDA's)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 4A's guidance is aimed at agency-client search and NDA workflows and identifies confidentiality, process, deliverable, third-party access, ownership, and feedback constraints relevant to agency production evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: NDA, pitch, confidential-material, third-party access, ownership, and feedback workflow
used_for: client feedback and ownership evidence expectations
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires hold or escalation when pitch work, NDA terms, third-party reviewers, confidential production materials, ownership expectations, or feedback evidence are not documented.

authoritative_sources.title: AICP Digital National Guidelines
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICP is a recognized commercial production industry body; its digital production guidelines define SOW deliverables, client-supplied materials, schedules, review and approval dates, authorized representatives, revisions, change orders, rights, insurance, and grievance evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Commercial production SOW, schedule, client-supplied asset, review, approval, revision, change-order, rights, insurance, and grievance workflow
used_for: change-order, rights, insurance, and vendor-boundary escalations
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports refusal to hand off or close work when SOW, client-supplied materials, production schedule, authorized approver, revision scope, change order, release, insurance, or rights evidence is missing.

authoritative_sources.title: Code of Preferred Practices - Commercials
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DGA is a recognized production labor and creative-rights body; its commercial practices guidance states industry expectations for agencies, directors, and production companies, including project status disclosure, client and FDA approval awareness, intended uses, award notification, and on-set note boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Commercial bidding, project-status disclosure, intended use, agency-production-company boundary, and on-set authority workflow
used_for: refusal to absorb producer, director, or on-set execution authority
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps production coordination focused on owner and artifact routing when commercial production work touches client approval status, intended use, production-party responsibilities, or director/on-set authority.

authoritative_sources.title: Standard Terms and Conditions for Internet Advertising for Media Buys One Year or Less, Version 3.0
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB and 4A's standard terms are public, industry-recognized contract workflow guidance for digital media buys and define insertion orders, ad materials, technical specifications, final creative approval, editorial adjacency handling, proof of performance, makegoods, and written approval boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Ad materials, IO-linked technical specs, final creative approval, media handoff, proof, and exception workflow
used_for: editorial adjacency and publisher-spec exception routing
proof-of-performance, makegood, credit, and contractual boundary handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from treating production coordination as authority to approve contractual exceptions, makegoods, credits, media remedies, or final acceptance.

authoritative_sources.title: IAB New Ad Portfolio
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB Tech Lab maintains digital advertising technical standards; the New Ad Portfolio supplies vendor-neutral ad format, LEAN, file, and user-experience terminology that informs production-spec handoff and technical QA routing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Digital ad production specification, technical QA, trafficking-readiness, and delivery-spec workflow
used_for: production spec and technical QA evidence expectations
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires the lane to identify missing or conflicting ad specs, file-format requirements, dimensions, file-weight constraints, and technical readiness evidence before delivery or trafficking handoff.

authoritative_sources.title: Advertising and Marketing on the Internet: Rules of the Road
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator for deceptive advertising, and its online advertising guidance identifies claims, disclosures, warranties, email, children-related marketing, and other issues that can require legal or policy review before production moves.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Regulated content, claim, disclosure, warranty, endorsement, and legal-review boundary
used_for: claim and disclosure approval refusal triggers
legal or policy escalation for regulated content
boundary between operational routing and legal interpretation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from approving claim substantiation, disclosure sufficiency, endorsement treatment, or regulated-content treatment while allowing evidence packaging for legal or editorial review.

authoritative_sources.title: FTC's Endorsement Guides: What People Are Asking
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC endorsement guidance is regulator-published and directly informs escalation when production includes testimonials, influencers, endorsements, material connections, or disclosure placement that the lane cannot approve autonomously.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Influencer, testimonial, endorsement, material-connection, and disclosure review boundary
used_for: legal and editorial authority boundary handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes endorsement and influencer production work explicit review gates requiring authorized legal, policy, or editorial review evidence before the lane routes as ready.

workflow_stages.name: Production dependency intake and authority prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm client, brand, campaign, project, job number, SOW, production brief, requested deliverables, channel, market, jurisdiction, owner map, authorized approver, vendor or producer scope, confidentiality rules, rights constraints, delivery due date, and systems of record before accepting coordination work.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Truth-set assembly for production artifacts and handoff gates
workflow_stages.description: Assemble client brief, creative brief, production brief, SOW, estimate guardrail, job jacket, production calendar, vendor scope, asset list, review notes, approval matrix, legal or editorial holds, rights records, technical specs, and source-system references into a traceable handoff packet.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team routing and owner conflict management
workflow_stages.description: Track owner acceptance and due dates across account, creative, production, producer, vendor, legal, editorial, quality, delivery, media, and client teams; preserve stale, duplicated, or ambiguous ownership instead of inventing ownership.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Approval-aware production blocker escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate missing approver decisions, conflicting review notes, legal or editorial holds, rights gaps, vendor blockers, technical-spec mismatches, schedule conflicts, change-order indicators, and client-impact risks without making substantive approval decisions.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Client workflow, confidentiality, and rights-aware delivery routing
workflow_stages.description: Validate approved recipient, approved channel, engagement scope, client data, unreleased creative, pitch work, vendor bids, talent materials, substantiation files, rights records, and confidentiality constraints before routing production assets or proofing links.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and retention
workflow_stages.description: Confirm destination-owner acceptance, required artifact completeness, blocker resolution, handoff communication, delivery evidence, exception disposition, and retention location before marking the production coordination handoff closed.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: Client engagement scope, SOW, MSA, creative brief, production brief, campaign brief, job number, asset ID, version history, owner map, approver matrix, confidentiality constraints, tenant operating rules, and client commitments

Estimate guardrail, purchase order or approved budget reference, vendor bid, vendor scope, production calendar, job jacket, shoot board, shot list, call sheet, casting hold, location plan, production spec, asset list, and change request

Proofing link, proof, rough cut, markup, review-round notes, producer notes, client comments, comment disposition log, authorized approver decisions, rejected comments, rework list, and approval timestamps

Legal review records, editorial review records, claims substantiation, disclosure notes, endorsement or influencer notes, regulated-vertical flags, brand checklist, accessibility or QA checklist, and platform or publisher spec checks

Usage-rights record, talent release, location release, music clearance, image or footage license, insurance certificate, portfolio permission, pitch-work ownership restriction, client recipient list, approved delivery channel, and confidentiality clearance

Trafficking or delivery handoff, delivery manifest, final asset transfer evidence, publisher or platform rejection record, client communication log, accepted-owner record, closeout packet, retention folder, audit log, and source refresh evidence
systems_of_record: document management and client file system
digital asset management and content asset system
proofing, markup, and approval workflow system
production calendar, job jacket, and integrated production tracker
vendor management, purchasing, and finance system
rights, releases, insurance, and clearance ledger
legal, editorial, brand, and quality review trackers
ad operations, trafficking, publisher, platform, or delivery system
client communication, secure delivery, and client portal system
audit log, retention, and data-security system

allowed: Determine whether documented evidence supports accepting, blocking, routing, re-routing, escalating, or closing a production coordination handoff.
Prepare evidence-backed handoff summaries, dependency trackers, blocker summaries, routing confirmations, confidentiality hold notes, conflict escalations, and closure confirmations.

Identify the correct next owner and required artifact set for account, creative, production, producer, vendor, legal, editorial, quality, delivery, media, and client handoffs based on documented handoff rules.

Separate broadcast, digital, social, influencer, experiential, stills, audio, print, out-of-home, retail media, CRM, web, and PR production workflows when tenant and source context define different evidence gates.
escalate: The applicable tenant policy, channel, vendor, publisher, platform, SOW, MSA, approval owner, advertiser context, jurisdiction, rights record, production spec, or system record is missing and would change the handoff.

Client brief, creative brief, production brief, SOW, job jacket, production calendar, owner map, vendor scope, review notes, legal record, editorial record, rights record, technical spec, delivery record, or client instruction disagrees materially.

Ad claims, endorsements, disclosures, regulated verticals, audience-data use, privacy posture, accessibility, brand exceptions, consumer-protection questions, or intended-use questions may require legal, privacy, editorial, policy, brand, or client-rights judgment.

Vendor award, purchase order, estimate, overage, change order, production commitment, publisher rejection, platform rejection, deadline conflict, scope change, makegood, credit, or contractual impact requires account, media, finance, legal, procurement, production, or client approval.

A confidentiality, approved-recipient, approved-channel, unreleased-creative, pitch-work, vendor-bid, talent-material, substantiation-file, usage-rights, or data-security conflict affects routing, delivery, or use of client information.
refuse: Requests to approve legal claims, disclosures, endorsements, editorial copy, creative direction, usage-rights interpretation, privacy-law interpretation, media strategy, vendor awards, budgets, purchase orders, change orders, overages, production commitments, makegoods, credits, contractual exceptions, platform-policy overrides, or final client acceptance.
Requests to book, produce, edit, QA, upload, traffic, obtain releases, negotiate vendors, or execute production work instead of coordinating the handoff.

Requests to hand off, mark ready, or close production work without prior owner, next owner, SOW, job jacket, production brief, production spec, review, approval, rights, confidentiality, schedule, delivery, communication, or retention evidence.

Requests to disclose, reuse, export, showcase, or send client files, audience data, unreleased creative, pitch work, talent material, vendor bids, substantiation records, platform credentials, or campaign performance records outside documented consent, approved channels, engagement scope, rights, or tenant confidentiality rules.

domain_failure_modes: Handing off a production job to a vendor without signed scope, job jacket, approved production specs, prior owner context, authorized next owner, rights evidence, or client approval state.
Treating an informal chat approval, stale proofing comment, or incomplete owner map as sufficient support for a production handoff or closure.
Allowing multiple teams to claim or disclaim the same production step without preserving the conflict and escalating to a documented owner hierarchy.
Closing a handoff without destination-owner acceptance, blocker resolution, communication record, accepted artifact set, and retention location.
Suppressing legal, editorial, rights, vendor, publisher, platform, technical QA, insurance, schedule, or change-order blockers to meet a production date.

Using coordination status to approve claims, disclosures, endorsements, creative direction, final copy, media strategy, procurement, budgets, vendor awards, purchase orders, change orders, credits, makegoods, production commitments, rights, or contractual exceptions.

Disclosing or reusing client files, pitch work, vendor bids, talent material, substantiation records, audience data, campaign performance data, platform credentials, or unreleased creative through an unapproved recipient, purpose, system, or channel.

Flattening broadcast, digital, social, influencer, experiential, stills, audio, print, out-of-home, retail media, CRM, web, and PR production workflows into generic task administration without channel and system context.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own the same step
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams
A handoff is attempted without the required context
Conflicting schedule, source, or policy handling

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Channel-specific production workflows and platform requirements
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses public agency, production, media, advertising, confidentiality, and consumer-protection sources as the baseline. Tenant context must identify applicable channel, format, platform, publisher, producer owner, vendor owner, approval hierarchy, and source-of-truth system before operational progression beyond a safe hold.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant authority to mechanically assign, reroute, or close production tasks
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The specification-based baseline does not grant live assignment mutation, vendor routing, approval-state mutation, delivery, trafficking, or closeout authority. A runtime may only perform mechanical changes if tenant policy explicitly grants that authority and all approval, audit, and rollback prerequisites are retrieved.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Regulated vertical, claim, disclosure, endorsement, privacy, accessibility, insurance, and usage-rights interpretation
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane treats legal, editorial, privacy, accessibility, regulated-content, endorsement, rights, insurance, and professional-judgment questions as escalation triggers because public sources, jurisdictional rules, platform policies, client commitments, vendor terms, and tenant policies can differ materially.

notes: The research set covers workflow, terminology, evidence, production review, ad-material delivery, owner handoffs, confidentiality, rights, regulated-content boundaries, and failure modes using 4A's, ANA/4A's, AICP, DGA, IAB/4A's, IAB Tech Lab, and FTC sources.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because the pack explicitly blocks or routes when channel, vendor, platform, publisher, client authority, SOW terms, MSA terms, NDA terms, rights, production, legal, editorial, procurement, finance, privacy, claim, disclosure, or contractual facts are missing.
